response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled individual contributor with broad polyglot experience across backend, frontend, and distributed systems, but he is fundamentally misaligned with a CTO role. He holds no executive or engineering-leadership titles, has no documented track record of scaling or managing engineering teams, and his only AI/agent experience amounts to roughly two months of MCP tooling work at his current employer. Fintech infrastructure depth is limited to a developer role at a small investment startup with no exposure to credit systems, real-time risk, or compliance engineering. The gap between his current Staff Software Engineer profile and the executive, agentic-AI, and fintech demands of this position is too wide to justify an interview.
strengths: 15+ years of polyglot backend experience spanning microservices, event-driven architectures, and high-throughput systems (Dunnhumby: millions of req/s)
Early hands-on exposure to agentic AI workflows and MCP tooling at re:cinq, showing awareness of the space
Founder of an open-source event-driven geo-data platform demonstrating end-to-end system ownership and long-term maintenance discipline
concerns: Zero executive or people-leadership experience — no evidence of team scaling, hiring authority, architectural governance, or board-level communication
AI and agentic-systems depth is superficial — only ~2 months of MCP developer tooling; no production multi-agent systems at commercial scale
No meaningful fintech infrastructure background — credit systems, payment flows, real-time risk modeling, and multi-jurisdiction compliance are entirely absent
